verdict.answer_first: B leads with the command-level outcome in sentence 1: "Truncate it in place"; A also answers early but opens with principle: "truncate the file in place".
verdict.decisiveness: B is more single-prescription: "The simplest way" then one command; A adds temp-tail, logrotate config, HUP pattern, and caveat branches, so it feels more like a survey.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute: ": > /path/to/logfile" plus "sudo truncate -s 0"; A is more complete but heavier and includes a risky-looking temp example.
